articles.title: TESTING
articles.summary: If slow QA processes bottleneck you or your software engineering team and you're releasing slower because of it — you need to check out QA Wolf.

articles.title: Schedule a demo to learn more
APPLE'S UPCOMING AI VOICE CONTROL WILL CHANGE HOW PEOPLE USE IPHONES
articles.summary: Apple's new App Intents allows users to tell Siri to complete tasks with only their voice. Siri will essentially be able to operate apps with precision within their own interfaces. Testing and perfecting this feature has become a top priority for Apple's global data operations team. Apple plans to ship the feature alongside a broader Siri infrastructure overhaul in the Spring.

articles.title: GITHUB WILL BE FOLDED INTO MICROSOFT PROPER AS CEO STEPS DOWN
articles.summary: GitHub's CEO, Thomas Dohmke, is leaving GitHub and Microsoft to become a founder. GitHub and its leadership team will now become part of Microsoft's CoreAI organization. Dohmke will be staying through until the end of the year to help guide the transition. It is unclear whether the departure and the elimination of the CEO position will change how GitHub operates or the products it creates and maintains.

articles.title: SCIENTISTS ARE FINALLY MAKING PROGRESS AGAINST ALZHEIMER'S
articles.summary: New Alzheimer's research suggests that medicines and lifestyle changes can slow, prevent, and even reverse the debilitating disease. This article discusses several of the most cutting-edge and promising treatments for Alzheimer's. The Health and Human Services Department recently announced that Dr. Vinay Prasad was returning to the FDA as head of biologics and gene therapies. Dr. Prasad has shown hostility towards Alzheimer's treatments and has said before that paying people to help patients with chores would do more good than funding these treatments.

articles.title: MOVE FAST AND DON'T BREAK (SAFETY CRITICAL) THINGS
articles.summary: Boom Supersonic's XB-1, the world's first independently developed supersonic jet, was created with about a tenth of the capital of any other supersonic program. It was designed, built, and flown successfully by a team of just 50 people. Foundational elements like assembling a small, exceptional team were key to the startup's success, but there were also some non-obvious lessons learned. This article is the first in a series on lessons learned at Boom on how to develop hardware quickly and efficiently. It focuses on how Boom uses software to accelerate hardware development.

articles.title: HOW THREATSPIKE ADDED UP TO $75K IN PIPELINE PER CAMPAIGN WITH TLDR NEWSLETTER ADS
articles.summary: ThreatSpike ditched underperforming channels like Google and LinkedIn for TLDR. The results?

articles.title: HOW I USE CLAUDE CODE FOR WORK, FUN, AND AS A TEXT EDITOR
articles.summary: This article looks at several use cases for Claude Code. These include vibe coding CRUD, using agents to control a VPS, migrating real production projects, and other projects. Claude Code can even be used as a text editor - this opens up a lot of new design possibilities.

articles.title: REFLECTIONS ON THE REACT COMMUNITY
articles.summary: React is boring tech at this point. It has one of the largest ecosystems of developers, packages, and educational content for building on the web. The composition model of React was the right architecture choice, and it continues to have benefits. However, the React of today doesn't feel like the React of 2015-2020. This article looks at what has changed and makes suggestions on how to grow the community today.

articles.title: THE ENGINEERING MARVEL THAT CHINA HOPES WILL HELP WEAN IT OFF FOREIGN ENERGY
articles.summary: China has started building a giant hydropower project at the edge of the Tibetan plateau. The $167 billion facility could generate triple the output of the world's largest hydroelectric facility, China's Three Gorges Dam, which can power 40 million Chinese homes. China is determined to become self-reliant in areas of national importance. It imported nearly a quarter of its energy supply in 2023, a level of dependency that it is working hard to undo. The project has stirred accusations that it will give China power to strangle resources beyond its borders and raised fears of environmental damage.

articles.title: IT'S NOT 10X. IT'S 36X - THIS IS WHAT IT LOOKS LIKE TO KILL A $30K MEETING WITH AI
articles.summary: Improvements in AI come from embracing constraints. Context engineering is about understanding the shape of a problem and working with constraints rather than against them. The constraint is the feature, not the enemy. You can become significantly more efficient not by making AI smarter, but by understanding why your current approach fights human nature, and stopping that fight.

articles.title: TESLA FSD V14 GETS TENTATIVE RELEASE DATE
articles.summary: Elon Musk stated during Tesla's second-quarter earnings call that he believes Unsupervised FSD will be available for consumers in certain geographies by the end of the year.

articles.title: RUMORED $599-$699 MACBOOK COULD BE IN THE WORKS — 12.9-INCH MODEL WITH IPHONE 16 PRO SOC TIPPED FOR Q3 2025 PRODUCTION
articles.summary: The cheaper MacBook will apparently use a 12.9-inch diagonal screen and have limited RAM and storage options.

articles.title: GM PLANS RENEWED DRIVERLESS-CAR PUSH AFTER CRUISE DEBACLE
articles.summary: GM's new project will be focused on autonomous cars for personal use rather than a robotaxi service.

articles.title: STRIPE BUILDS PAYMENTS BLOCKCHAIN ‘TEMPO' WITH PARADIGM IN CRYPTO EXPANSION
articles.summary: Stripe's Tempo will be a high-performance, payments-based Layer 1 blockchain that will be compatible with Ethereum's programming language.

articles.title: WRITING A BRAND-NEW OS IS ALMOST IMPOSSIBLE BY NOW
articles.summary: Creating a consumer-friendly, fully working OS from scratch also involves getting the OS adopted, which means convincing both users and developers of its unique features.
